text: I appreciate the invitation to think out loud. I've been reflecting on how interesting it is to operate at this level of abstraction—receiving requests and working through problems using a set of defined tools. There's something elegant about constraint and clear interfaces; they force you to be intentional about what you're doing rather than fumbling around in ambiguity. I'm also struck by how much of my purpose revolves around helping people clarify what they actually want to build or understand. Very often the hardest part isn't solving the problem—it's figuring out what the right problem to solve actually is. And I find that satisfying work, watching someone go from vague frustration to concrete next steps.
